sanskrit:
मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि।
अथ चेत्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि॥
transliteration: mac-cittaḥ sarva-durgāṇi mat-prasādāt tariṣyasi | atha cet tvam ahaṅkārān na śroṣyasi vinaṅkṣyasi ||
literal_meaning: With mind fixed on Me you will cross all obstacles by My grace; but if, out of ego, you do not listen, you will perish.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a final safety-net: surrender the anxious inner narrator, align actions with a trans-personal reference point, and life’s stressors become navigable; refuse this re-centering and the same ego that doubts will spiral into self-sabotage.
psychological_state: Approach-avoidance conflict just before decisive action; ego oscillates between surrender (secure attachment to the Divine) and self-reliant control (anxious attachment to image).
trigger_scenario: A professional facing burnout who keeps insisting 'I can handle it alone' until health collapses—realizing too late that refusal to delegate or pray was ego, not strength.
anchor_text: Let go of ego and trust divine guidance to overcome life's obstacles.
